ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Conference
Off Calendar Oct-06-2020 Off Calendar

Petition For Final Distribution; Transfer Of Real Property To Petitioner; Equalization Of Shares Of Trust; Breach Of Duty; Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-07-2020 Continued To Oct-06-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Bridgman appeared by telephone for Christian Andreasen (Petitioner).
David Sauers appeared by telephone for Heidi Welch (Trustee).

Counsel provided history and spoke of the issues with regard to the Trustee refusing to distribute the trust property, receipts from the Laye Trust not being signed by Heidi, the issue of payment of the equalization amount, Heidi not being the trustee of the Laye Trust, but a beneficiary and not having any obligation to sign any Laye Trust documents, Christian having taken over $250,000.00 from the Mother's bank accounts, Christian's position that he was entitled to the money because he was providing care for his Mother and that some amounts were gifts, etc. The Court informed Counsel that it is not addressing anything in the Laye Trust and that if the Parties want anything in the Laye Trust they will have to file a petition in that trust. Mr. Bridgman stated that this trust does qualify for the Court's mediation program. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than August 28, 2020. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by September 11, 2020. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on October 6, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The Court also signed a case management order. The case management hearing is set for October 6, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to file a joint case management at least 15-days prior to that date. The Court informed Counsel that it is the Petitioner's responsibility to serve the case management order and file a proof of service of the order. Counsel are to obtain copies of both orders through the Court's website.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.
